title: rosa coriifolia var. cinerea
intro: Rosa coriifolia var. cinerea, also known as the Ashy Rose, is a plant species in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its ash-gray leaves and pink to white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Rosa coriifolia var. cinerea has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its flowers and leaves, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
